lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where did you say I could find your son Caleb again?

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: The only thing I have heard of my son Caleb is that he is trying to earn his fortune as a great chef in the lands beyond White Wolf Mountain.

sections.title: Talking to Dimintheis again before acquiring all crest parts

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: Have you any news on my crest yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still looking for it.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: Hello. My name is Dimintheis, of the noble family Fitzharmon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why would a nobleman live in a dump like this?

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: The King has taken my estate from me until such time as I can show my family crest to him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why would he do that?

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: Well, there is a long standing rule of chivalry amongst the Varrockian aristocracy, where each noble family is in possession of a unique crest, which signifies the honour and lineage of the family. More than this

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: however, it also represents the lawful rights of each family to prove their ownership of their wealth and lands. If the family crest is lost, then the family's estate is handed over to the current monarch until the crest is

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: restored.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: This dates back to the times when there was much in- fighting amongst the noble families and their clans, and was introduced as a way of reducing the bloodshed that was devestating the ranks of the ruling classes at that

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: time. When you captured a rival family's clan, you also captured their lands and wealth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So where is this crest?

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: Well, my three sons took it with them many years ago when they rode out to fight in the war against the undead necromancer and his army in the battle to save Varrock.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: For many years I had assumed them all dead, as I had heard no word from them, but recently I heard word that my son Caleb is alive and well, and trying to earn his fortune as a great chef in the lands beyond White

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: Wolf Mountain, although I know not where.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I will help you.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: I thank you greatly adventurer! Also... if you find Caleb... please... let him know his father still loves him...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not interested in that adventure right now.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: I realise it was a lot to ask of a stranger.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're rich then? Can I have some money?

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: Gah! Lousy Beggar! Your sort is what's ruining this great land! Why don't you just go and get a job if you need the money so badly?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, I am a bold adventurer.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: An adventurer hmmm? How lucky. I may have an adventure for you. I desperately need my family crest returning to me. It is of utmost importance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are you so desperate for it?

lines.speaker: Caleb
lines.text: How is the fish collecting going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I didn't manage to get them all yet...

lines.speaker: Caleb
lines.text: Remember, I want the following cooked fish: Swordfish, Bass, Tuna, Salmon, and Shrimp.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Got them all with me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you very much.

lines.speaker: Caleb
lines.text: You're welcome.

sections.title: Talking to Caleb again after obtaining the first crest piece

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where did you say I could find Avan again?

lines.speaker: Caleb
lines.text: Last I heard he was on some stupid hunt for gold or treasure, somewhere in the desert east of Lumbridge. You might want to ask the traders there about him.

lines.speaker: Caleb
lines.text: How are you doing getting the crest pieces?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am still working on it.

lines.speaker: Caleb
lines.text: Then why are you wasting your time here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have lost the piece you gave me...

lines.speaker: Caleb
lines.text: I have some good news for you then. One of my customers found this on their travels and recognised it as mine and returned it to me here.

lines.speaker: Caleb
lines.text: I suggest you be less careless in the future. The crest is extremely valuable, and utterly irreplaceable.

sections.title: Talking to Caleb again after giving the fish (duplicate in case this line comes up later)

lines.speaker: Caleb
lines.text: Hello again. I'm just putting the finishing touches to my masterful salad.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good luck with that then.

lines.speaker: Caleb
lines.text: Luck? LUCK? With my cooking skill, I don't really need LUCK.

sections.title: Talking to Caleb after assembling the family crest

lines.speaker: Caleb
lines.text: How are you doing getting the crest pieces?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got it right here!

lines.speaker: Caleb
lines.text: Excellent work! Please take it to my father immediately.

lines.speaker: Caleb
lines.text: Who are you? What are you after?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you Caleb Fitzharmon?

lines.speaker: Caleb
lines.text: Why... yes I am, but I don't believe I know you... how did you know my name?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have been sent by your father. He wishes the Fitzharmon Crest be restored.

lines.speaker: Caleb
lines.text: Ah... well... hmmm... yes... I do have a piece of it anyway...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh... what happened to the rest of it?

lines.speaker: Caleb
lines.text: Well... my brothers and I had a slight disagreement about it... we all wanted to be heir to my fathers' lands, and we each ended up with a piece of the crest.

lines.speaker: Caleb
lines.text: None of us wanted to give up our rights to our brothers, so we didn't want to give up our pieces of the crest, but none of us wanted to face our father by

lines.speaker: Caleb
lines.text: returning to him with an incomplete crest... We each went our seperate ways many years past, none of us seeing our father or willing to give up our fragments.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So do you know where I could find any of your brothers?

lines.speaker: Caleb
lines.text: Well, we haven't really kept in touch... what with the dispute over the crest and all... I did hear from my brother Avan a while ago though.

lines.speaker: Caleb
lines.text: He said he was on some kind of search for treasure, or gold, or something out in the desert east of Lumbridge. You might want to ask the traders there about him.

lines.speaker: Caleb
lines.text: Avan always did have expensive tastes however. You may find he is not prepared to hand over his crest piece to you as easily as I have.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So can I have your bit?

lines.speaker: Caleb
lines.text: Well, I am the oldest son, so by the rules of chivalry, I am most entitled to be rightful bearer of the crest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's not really much use without the other fragments is it though?

lines.speaker: Caleb
lines.text: Well that is true... perhaps it is time to put my pride aside... I'll tell you what: I'm struggling to complete this fish salad of mine,

lines.speaker: Caleb
lines.text: so if you will assist me in my search for the ingredients, then I will let you take my piece as reward for your assistance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what ingredients are you missing?

lines.speaker: Caleb
lines.text: I require the following cooked fish: Swordfish, Bass, Tuna, Salmon and Shrimp.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I will get those.

lines.speaker: Caleb
lines.text: You will? It would help me a lot!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why don't you just give me the crest?

lines.speaker: Caleb
lines.text: It's a valuable family heirloom. I think the least you can do is prove you're worthy of it before I hand it over.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing, I will be on my way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see you are a chef, will you cook me anything?

lines.speaker: Caleb
lines.text: I would, but I am very busy. I am trying to increase my reknown as one of the world's leading chefs by preparing a special and unique fish salad.

lines.speaker: Silk trader
lines.text: Do you want to buy any fine silks?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm in search of a man named Avan Fitzharmon.

lines.speaker: Silk trader
lines.text: Can't say as I've seen him... I'm sure if he's been to Al Kharid recently someone around here will have though.

lines.speaker: Zeke
lines.text: A thousand greetings, sir.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm in search of a man named Avan Fitzharmon.

lines.speaker: Zeke
lines.text: Can't say as I've seen him... I'm sure if he's been to Al Kharid recently someone around here will have though.

lines.speaker: Karim
lines.text: Would you like to buy a nice kebab? Only one gold.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm in search of a man named Avan Fitzharmon.

lines.speaker: Karim
lines.text: Can't say as I've seen him... I'm sure if he's been to Al Kharid recently someone around here will have though.

lines.speaker: Shop keeper
lines.text: Can I help you at all?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm in search of a man named Avan Fitzharmon.

lines.speaker: Shop keeper
lines.text: Can't say as I've seen him... I'm sure if he's been to Al Kharid recently someone around here will have though.

lines.speaker: Louie Legs
lines.text: Hey, wanna buy some armour?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm in search of a man named Avan Fitzharmon.

lines.speaker: Louie Legs
lines.text: Can't say as I've seen him... I'm sure if he's been to Al Kharid recently someone around here will have though.

lines.speaker: Ranael
lines.text: Do you want to buy any armoured skirts? Designed especially for ladies who like to fight.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm in search of a man named Avan Fitzharmon.

lines.speaker: Ranael
lines.text: Can't say as I've seen him... I'm sure if he's been to Al Kharid recently someone around here will have though.

sections.title: Talking to Avan (Man) before talking to the Gem trader

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: What? Can't you see I'm busy?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, sooooorry...

lines.speaker: Gem trader
lines.text: Good day to you traveller. Would you be interested in buying some gems?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm in search of a man named Avan Fitzharmon.

lines.speaker: Gem trader
lines.text: Fitzharmon eh? Hmmm.... If I'm not mistaken that's the family name of a member of the Varrockian nobility. You know, I HAVE seen someone of that

lines.speaker: Gem trader
lines.text: persuasion around here recently... Wearing a poncey yellow cape he was! Came in here all la-di-dah, high and mighty, asking for jewelry made

lines.speaker: Gem trader
lines.text: from 'perfect gold' - whatever that is - like 'normal' golds just not good enough for little lord fancy pants there! I told him to head to the desert

lines.speaker: Gem trader
lines.text: 'Cos I know theres gold out there, in them there sand dunes... and if it's not up to his lordships high standards of 'gold perfection'...

lines.speaker: Gem trader
lines.text: Maybe we'll all get lucky and the scorpions will deal with him.

lines.speaker: Avan
lines.text: So how are you doing getting me my perfect gold jewelry?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still after that 'perfect gold'.

lines.speaker: Avan
lines.text: I know how you feel... for such a long time I have searched and searched for the elusive perfect gold... I thought I had gotten a good lead on finding it

lines.speaker: Avan
lines.text: when I heard talk of a dwarven expert on gold named Boot some time back, but unfortunately for me, he has returned to his mountain home, which I cannot find.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are you lurking around a scorpion pit?

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: It's a good place to find gold...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for a man... his name is Avan Fitzharmon.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Then you have found him. My name is Avan Fitzharmon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You have a part of your family crest. I am on a quest to retrieve all of the fragmented pieces and restore the crest.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Ha! I suppose one of my worthless brothers has sent you on this quest then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, it was your father who has asked me to do this for him.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: My... my father wishes this? Then that is a different matter. I will let you have my crest shard, adventurer, but you must first do something for me.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: There is a certain lady I am trying to impress. As a man of noble birth, I can not give her just any old trinket to show my devotion. What I intend to give her,

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: is a golden ring, embedded with the finest precious red stone available, and a necklace to match this ring. The problem however for me, is that

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: not just any old gold will be suitable. I seek only the purest, the most high quality of gold - what I seek, if you will, is perfect gold.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: None of the gold around here is even remotely suitable in terms of quality. I have searched far and wide for the perfect gold I desire, but have had no success so

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: in finding it I am afraid. If you can find me my perfect gold, make a ring and necklace from it, and add rubies to them, I will

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: gladly hand over my fragment of the family crest to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you give me any help on finding this 'perfect gold'?

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: I thought I had found a solid lead on its whereabouts when I heard of a dwarf who is an expert on gold who goes by the name of 'Boot'.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Unfortunately he has apparently returned to his home, somewhere in the mountains, and I have no idea how to find him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I'll see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Boot
lines.text: Hello tall person.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. I'm in search of very high quality gold.

lines.speaker: Boot
lines.text: High quality gold eh? Hmmm... Well, the very best quality gold that I know of, is just East of the large city Ardougne, underground somewhere.

lines.speaker: Boot
lines.text: I don't believe it's exactly easy to get to though...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello short person.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are you called boot?

lines.speaker: Boot
lines.text: I'm called Boot, because when I was very young, I used to sleep, in a large boot.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, great, I didn't want your life story.

sections.title: Talking To Avan after talking to Boot
If the player has not made the jewellery

lines.speaker: Avan
lines.text: So how are you doing making me my perfect gold jewelry?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have spoken to Boot the dwarf about the location of 'perfect gold' but haven't managed to make you your jewelry yet.

lines.speaker: Avan
lines.text: Well, I won't entrust you with my piece of the crest until you have brought me a necklace of perfect gold with a red precious stone, and a perfect gold ring to match.

sections.title: If the player has the jewellery

lines.speaker: Avan
lines.text: So how are you doing making me my perfect gold jewelry?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have the ring and necklace right here.

lines.speaker: Avan
lines.text: These... these are exquisite! EXACTLY what I was searching for all of this time! Please, take my crest fragment!

lines.speaker: Avan
lines.text: Now, I suppose you will be wanting to find my brother Johnathon who is in possession of the final piece of the family's crest?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's correct.

lines.speaker: Avan
lines.text: Well, the last I heard of my brother Johnathon, he was studying the magical arts, and trying to hunt some demon or other out in The Wilderness.

lines.speaker: Avan
lines.text: Unsurprisingly, I do not believe he is doing a particularly good job of things, and spends most of his time recovering from his injuries in

lines.speaker: Avan
lines.text: some tavern or other near the edge of The Wilderness. You'll probably find him still there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks Avan.

sections.title: Talking to Avan after getting his crest piece

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where did you say I could find your brother Johnathon again?

lines.speaker: Avan
lines.text: Well, the last I heard of my brother Johnathon, he was studying the magical arts, and trying to hunt some demon or other out in The Wilderness.

lines.speaker: Avan
lines.text: Unsurprisingly, I do not believe he is doing a particularly good job of things, and spends most of his time recovering from his injuries in

lines.speaker: Avan
lines.text: some tavern or other near the edge of The Wilderness. You'll probably find him still there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks Avan.

sections.title: Talking to Avan after obtaining all crest pieces

lines.speaker: Avan
lines.text: Greetings again, adventurer. How are you doing on retrieving the crest pieces?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am still working on it.

lines.speaker: Avan
lines.text: I hope you succeed for my father's sake.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have lost the fragment you gave me...

lines.speaker: Avan
lines.text: I have a confession myself adventurer... I did not fully trust you with the actual part of my family's crest before, and gave you a worthless replica before...

lines.speaker: Avan
lines.text: In hindsight, it seems I was right. I will give you the real piece now, but PLEASE try not to lose it; it is a priceless family heirloom.

sections.title: Talking to Avan after assembling the family crest

lines.speaker: Avan
lines.text: Greetings again, adventurer. How are you doing on retrieving the crest pieces?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have assembled all of the pieces!

lines.speaker: Avan
lines.text: Very well done adventurer! Now please return it to my father!

lines.speaker: Johnathon
lines.text: What... what did that spider... DO to me? I... I feel so weak... I can hardly... think at all...

lines.speaker: Johnathon
lines.text: Ooooh... thank you... Wow! That's completely cured me!

lines.speaker: Johnathon
lines.text: How can I reward you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've come here for your piece of the Fitzharmon family crest.

lines.speaker: Johnathon
lines.text: You have? Unfortunately I don't have it any more... in my attempts to slay the fiendish Chronozon, the blood demon, I lost a lot of equipment in

lines.speaker: Johnathon
lines.text: our last battle when he bested me and forced me away from his den. He probably still has it now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So is this Chronozon hard to defeat?

lines.speaker: Johnathon
lines.text: Well... you will have to be a skilled Mage to defeat him, and my powers are not good enough yet. You will need to hit him once with each of the four elemental spells of death

lines.speaker: Johnathon
lines.text: before he will be defeated.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find Chronozon?

lines.speaker: Johnathon
lines.text: The fiend has made his lair in the Wilderness below the Obelisk of Air.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how did you end up getting poisoned?

lines.speaker: Johnathon
lines.text: Those accursed poison spiders that surround the entrance to Chronozon's lair... I must have taken a nip from one of them as I attempted to make my escape.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will be on my way now.

lines.speaker: Johnathon
lines.text: My thanks for the assistance adventurer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm trying to kill this demon Chronozon that you mentioned...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wish me luck.

lines.speaker: Johnathon
lines.text: Good luck. You'll need it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have your piece of the crest!

lines.speaker: Johnathon
lines.text: Well done! Now return it to my father!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Greetings. Would you happen to be Johnathon Fitzharmon?

lines.speaker: Johnathon
lines.text: That... I am...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am here to retrieve your fragment of the Fitzharmon family crest.

lines.speaker: Johnathon
lines.text: The.. poison.. it is all.. too much... My head... will not... stop spinning...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have retrieved your crest.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: Adventurer... I can only thank you for your kindness, although the words are insufficient to express the gratitude I feel!

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: You are truly a hero in every sense, and perhaps your efforts can begin to patch the wounds that have torn this family apart...

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: I know not how I can adequately reward you for your efforts... although I do have these mystical gauntlets, a family heirloom that through some power unknown to me, have always returned to the head of the family

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: whenever lost, or if the owner has died. I will pledge these to you, and if you should lose them return to me, and they will be here.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: They can also be granted extra powers. Take them to one of my sons, they should be able to imbue them with a skill for you.

sections.title: Talking to Caleb after the quest

lines.speaker: Caleb
lines.text: I hear you brought the completed crest to my father. I must say, that was awfully impressive work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I believe your father mentioned you would be able to improve these gauntlets for me...

lines.speaker: Caleb
lines.text: Yes, that is correct. I can slightly alter these gauntlets to allow you some of my skill at preparing seafood by making them cooking gauntlets.

lines.speaker: Caleb
lines.text: When you wear them you will burn lobsters, sharks and swordfish a lot less. It will be a permanent modification however, so make sure this is what you want.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, please do that for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see what your brothers have to offer first.

lines.speaker: Caleb
lines.text: As you wish. Let me know if you change your mind.

sections.title: Talking to Avan after the quest

lines.speaker: Avan
lines.text: I have received word from my father of your assistance to our family in this matter. You have my thanks for restoring our honour.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your father mentioned that you could improve these gauntlets in some way for me...

lines.speaker: Avan
lines.text: Indeed I can. In my long time searching for the 'perfect' gold I learned a lot about gold items. I can improve these gauntlets so that they provide more than normal

lines.speaker: Avan
lines.text: experience when smithing gold. I can easily modify these for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That sounds good, improve them for me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I'll check my other options with your brothers before committing myself.

lines.speaker: Avan
lines.text: If you feel you really must consort with the likes of them. I await your decision.

sections.title: Talking to Johnathon after the quest

lines.speaker: Johnathon
lines.text: Hello again. My family now considers you a hero.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your father tells me you can improve these gauntlets for me...

lines.speaker: Johnathon
lines.text: And right he is at that. Although my skill with Death Rune magiks was not great enough to defeat Chronozon, I am adequately skilled at Chaos Rune magiks.

lines.speaker: Johnathon
lines.text: If you give me your gauntlets I will bestow upon them some of my power, and make any bolt spells you wish to cast more effective.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That sounds good to me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I'll check my other options with your brothers before committing myself.

lines.speaker: Johnathon
lines.text: I doubt they will offer anything as useful as my magical boost, but as you wish.
